revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:藤の森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.563736|N|135.604578|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Fujinomori Kofun |image = [[File:Fujinomori Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|thumb|Relocated stone chamber (within the grounds of {{ill|AICEL Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/AICEL Shura Hall|13=User:Immanuelle/AICEL Shura Hall|qq=}})]] |alternate_name = |cluster = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera City|1=Fujiidera}} Nonaka 1-chome
(within the Biryou Pump Station of {{ill|Osaka Metropolitan Waterworks Enterprise|ja|大阪広域水道企業団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Metropolitan Waterworks Enterprise|1=Osaka Water Supply Authority|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11440922}}) |coordinates = 49.45 |lon_deg = 135 |lon_min = 36 |lon_sec = 16.48 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Circular Tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter 25.5 m |burial = Right-sleeve type {{ill|Horizontal Cave Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal Cave Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with wooden coffin inside) |burial goods = Beads・Iron arrowheads・Iron spearhead・Leather-bound short armor・Horse gear・{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |auxiliary_mound = |built = Latter half of the {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |interred_person = |tumulus = |historic_site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = One of the oldest examples of a horizontal cave stone chamber in the Kinai region
The mound is no longer extant |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = top }} '''Fujinomori Kofun''' (ふじのもりこふん) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Nonaka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera City|1=Fujiidera}}. Its type was a {{ill|Circular Tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It was one of the kofun constituting the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is known as one of the oldest examples of a {{ill|Horizontal Cave Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal Cave Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the Kinai region; however, the mound is no longer extant. == Overview == [[File:Fujinomori Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the relocated stone chamber}}]] [[File:Fujinomori Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the relocated stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, this kofun was built on a plateau beyond a valley typeed by the Daijō River to the west of {{ill|Yodagoryōzan Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodagoryōzan Kofun|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11631432}} (the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}){{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. It has been subject to grave robbing, and a third excavation survey has been conducted{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Exhibition Explanation Materials)|2019}}. == Excavation History == * {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 40), excavation survey conducted in conjunction with the construction of the Biryou Pump Station of the Osaka Prefecture Waterworks Department (report published by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education in 1965). * {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 5), excavation survey (summary report by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education in 1993). * {{ill|1996|en|1996|qid=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8), excavation survey (summary report by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education in 1996). == burial == [[File:藤の森古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250x250px|{{center|burial goods}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|AICEL Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/AICEL Shura Hall|qq=}}.}}]] As a burial facility, a right-sleeve type {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was constructed with an opening facing south. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Exhibition Explanation Materials)|2019}}. * Entire chamber length: approximately 4.5 meters{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Main Chamber: length 3.5 meters, width 1.5 meters * Approach Passage: length approximately 1 meter, width 0.8–0.9 meters The stone of the chamber is composed of slab-like split rock{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Exhibition Explanation Materials)|2019}}, and the same material was used for the blocking stones{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The side walls of the main chamber were notably constructed by carrying in stones{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Red pigment coating is observed on the walls of the main chamber and the approach passage (but not on the blocking stones){{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Inside the chamber, black soil was filled from the base up to a height of 0.1 meters, and ballast was laid on top to type the floor. For the blockage of the approach passage, 5–6 courses of small stones were piled on the first course of slab stones, and additional earthen embankment was added on the outer surface of the blocking stones. Inside the chamber, the detection of iron nails and iron tie bars suggests that a wooden coffin was placed near the west wall{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The head position is presumed to be toward the north. Although the chamber has been subject to grave robbing, some grave goods were detected during the excavation survey{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}; among these, glass beads and round glass beads were concentrated in the northern and central parts of the chamber, and near the rear wall, a group of iron arrowheads, which appear to be in situ, was found. After the construction of the Biryou Pump Station, this stone chamber was relocated within the pump station; it is currently preserved on the grounds of {{ill|Aiselsura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiselsura Hall|1=AICEL Shura Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11282212}} (in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera City|1=Fujiidera}}, Fujiidera){{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Exhibition Explanation Materials)|2019}}. Fujinomori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main Chamber (rear wall direction) Fujinomori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main Chamber (entrance direction) == burial goods == The grave goods uncovered during the excavation survey are as follows{{Sfn|Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Exhibition Explanation Materials)|2019}}. * Glass magatama * Glass round beads * Bronze three-lobed beads * Iron arrowheads * Iron spearhead * Leather-bound short armor * Bit – excavated from the mound Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at {{ill|Aiselsura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiselsura Hall|1=AICEL Shura Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11282212}} (in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera City|1=Fujiidera}}, Fujiidera). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Aiselsura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|lt=Fujiidera City Lifelong Learning Center (Aiselsura Hall)|lt_ja=藤井寺市立生涯学習センター（アイセル シュラ ホール）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiselsura Hall|1=AICEL Shura Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11282212}} (in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera City|1=Fujiidera}}, Fujiidera) – Preserves and exhibits the relocated stone chamber and excavated burial goods of the Fujinomori Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used in the writing of this article)}} * Historic Site explanatory panel ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera City|1=Fujiidera}}, installed in 2019) * Publications by local governments ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Fujinomori Kofun|title=Fujiidera City History|volume=Volume 3, Materials Section 1|publisher=Fujiidera City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Fujinomori Kofun (Fujiidera City History)|1986}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Aiselsura Hall Exhibition Explanation Materials)" (Fujiidera City World Heritage Registration Promotion Office, 2019)|ref={{Harvid|Fujinomori Kofun and burial goods (Exhibition Explanation Materials)|2019}}}} * Reference works ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Fujinomori Kofun|title=Place names of Osaka Prefecture|series={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Series|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}}28|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Fujinomori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Michio Harada|year=1989|chapter=Matsudakeyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Fujinomori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Works == {{small|(Literature related to the article but not used in its composition)}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=Investigation of the Fujinomori and Banjozan Kofun in Biryou Town, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Waterworks Department|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=|title=Outline of the Excavation Survey of the Hazamiyama Site within the Biryou Pump Station of the Osaka Prefecture Waterworks Department – Investigation of Fujinomori Kofun –|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=|title=Outline of the Excavation Survey of the Hazamiyama Site within the Biryou Pump Station of the Osaka Prefecture Waterworks Department – Investigation of Fujinomori Kofun –|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} === Related Topics === {{Commonscat|Fujinomori Kofun}} * {{ill|Miyamaizuka Kofun|ja|宮山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamaizuka Kofun|1=Miyayamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65261827}} and {{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidayama Kofun|1=Takaidazan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668715}} – Kofun in the Kinai region with early horizontal cave stone chambers similar to Fujinomori Kofun. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] [[Template:infobox ancient site]] [[File:Fujinomori Kofun, sekishitsu-1-2.jpg|280px]] {{translated page|ja|藤の森古墳|version=101059107|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:藤の森古墳]] {{Commons category|Fujinomori Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11622625}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
